entries.text: What are the 9 navigation sub-functions in DNV-CG-0264 §4? Are these functions meant to cover the entire autonomous ship navigation system (all layers), or just one tactical layer? Specifically, does §4.2 'Planning prior to voyage' and §4.9 'Docking' belong to the tactical decision layer, or to voyage planning / control layers?

entries.text: In DNV-CG-0264 chapter 4, what are the exact 9 navigation sub-functions (§4.2 through §4.10)? Does DNV-CG-0264 define these as functions for the ENTIRE autonomous ship system (covering all decision layers from voyage planning to motion control), or specifically for a tactical/collision avoidance layer? Are §4.2 'Planning prior to voyage' and §4.9 'Docking' meant to be implemented in a tactical layer, or in voyage planning and motion control layers respectively?
keywords: In DNV-CG-0264 chapter 4, what are the exact 9 navigation sub-functions (§4.2 through §4.10)? Are §4.2 'Planning prior to voyage' and §4.9 'Docking' meant to be implemented in a tactical layer, or in voyage planning and motion control layers respectively?

entries.text: 学术COLAV主流路线对比(2020-2025): MPC-based避碰(BC-MPC、Model Predictive COLAV)、Velocity Obstacle方法(VO、GPVO、Dynamic VO)、IvP Helm行为仲裁、强化学习避碰(RL-based)。关键问题: (1)各方法在COLREGs Rules 13-17的合规性证明能力；(2)SIL 2安全认证可接受度；(3)避碰时间阈值(T_standOn、T_act、TCPA)的量化标准；(4)近5年综述文献。

entries.text: Verify 2026-05 technical baselines for marine SIL 2 / IEC 61508 / CCS i-Ship project using ROS2 (8 modules, C++17 + Python 3.10): (1) ROS2 LTS recommended for industrial/safety-critical use 2026 — Humble vs Iron vs Jazzy vs Kilted, EOL dates, default rmw vendor; (2) C++17 toolchain — GCC and Clang stable versions 2026; CMake recommended ver; colcon recommended ver; (3) Coding-standard adoption: AUTOSAR C++14 (MISRA merger 2023) vs MISRA C++:2008 vs MISRA C++:2023 — current state, vendor support (clang-tidy / Polyspace / Coverity rule packs), marine SIL 2 / DAL B precedent; (4) Static analysis tool versions 2026: Polyspace Code Prover / Coverity / clang-tidy / cppcheck — ROS2 / SIL 2 integration practice; (5) DDS impl for ROS2: Cyclone DDS vs Fast DDS vs RTI Connext — defaults, SIL certification readiness; (6) Library latest stable versions 2026: CasADi, IPOPT, GeographicLib, Eigen, nlohmann::json, Boost, GTest, spdlog; (7) GitLab CI pipeline schema 2026 + best practices for embedded/marine projects. Return structured table per item: name | version-in-2026 | citation | confidence.
keywords: Verify 2026-05 technical baselines for marine SIL 2 / IEC 61508 / CCS i-Ship project using ROS2 (8 modules, C++17 + Python 3.10): (1) ROS2 LTS recommended for industrial/safety-critical use 2026 — Humble vs Iron vs Jazzy vs Kilted, EOL dates, default rmw vendor; (2) C++17 toolchain — GCC and Clang stable versions 2026; CMake recommended ver; colcon recommended ver; (3) Coding-standard adoption: AUTOSAR C++14 (MISRA merger 2023) vs MISRA C++:2008 vs MISRA C++:2023 — current state, vendor support (clang-tidy / Polyspace / Coverity rule packs), marine SIL 2 / DAL B precedent; (4) Static analysis tool versions 2026: Polyspace Code Prover / Coverity / clang-tidy / cppcheck — ROS2 / SIL 2 integration practice; (5) DDS impl for ROS2: Cyclone DDS vs Fast DDS vs RTI Connext — defaults, SIL certification readiness; (6) Library latest stable versions 2026: CasADi, IPOPT, GeographicLib, Eigen, nlohmann::json, Boost, GTest, spdlog; (7) GitLab CI pipeline schema 2026 + best practices for embedded/marine projects. Return structured table per item: name | version-in-2026 | citation | confidence.

entries.text: L3 TDL SIL framework Option D Hybrid: full DNV toolchain (maritime-schema + ship-traffic-generator + farn + ospx + libcosim FMI/OSP + mlfmu) wrapping ROS2 Jazzy SIL kernel for CCS i-Ship N AiP submission. Evaluate certification-first all-in adoption vs incremental schema-only adoption for FCB 45m semi-planing vessel, 8-month plan. Specific questions: (1) Real engineering effort to integrate libcosim/OSP with ROS2 Jazzy node graph - documented FMI<->ROS2 bridges, latency/jitter penalties? (2) farn + ospx maturity for 1100-cell parametric case-folder generation in CI vs custom runner? (3) mlfmu ONNX-to-FMU pipeline production-readiness 2025-2026, RL policy export limitations? (4) maritime-schema v0.0.x stability - breaking changes risk, schema governance, industrial submission usage? (5) CCS DNV Brinav 2024 MoU - concrete evidence acceptance scope, has any Chinese AiP review actually accepted DNV maritime-schema? (6) Comparable projects with full DNV stack (NTNU SFI-AutoShip, PyGemini, Yara Birkeland, Brinav Zhifei) - what worked, what they regretted, what they forked. (7) ROS2 Jazzy + Ubuntu 22.04 compatibility 2026 - Humble vs Jazzy decision impact on FMI tooling. (8) Tool qualification for libcosim/farn/mlfmu - DNV-RP-0513 self-assurance evidence available? Recommendation: which subset is MUST-have for CCS AiP credibility vs nice-to-have, realistic incremental effort delta over schema-only Option B. Counter-evidence: any tool abandoned, showstopper bugs, or DNV/CCS rejected submissions?
keywords: L3 TDL SIL framework Option D Hybrid: full DNV toolchain (maritime-schema + ship-traffic-generator + farn + ospx + libcosim FMI/OSP + mlfmu) wrapping ROS2 Jazzy SIL kernel for CCS i-Ship N AiP submission. Evaluate certification-first all-in adoption vs incremental schema-only adoption for FCB 45m semi-planing vessel, 8-month plan. Specific questions: (1) Real engineering effort to integrate libcosim/OSP with ROS2 Jazzy node graph - documented FMI<->ROS2 bridges, latency/jitter penalties? (2) farn + ospx maturity for 1100-cell parametric case-folder generation in CI vs custom runner? (3) mlfmu ONNX-to-FMU pipeline production-readiness 2025-2026, RL policy export limitations? (4) maritime-schema v0.0.x stability - breaking changes risk, schema governance, industrial submission usage? (5) CCS DNV Brinav 2024 MoU - concrete evidence acceptance scope, has any Chinese AiP review actually accepted DNV maritime-schema? (6) Comparable projects with full DNV stack (NTNU SFI-AutoShip, PyGemini, Yara Birkeland, Brinav Zhifei) - what worked, what they regretted, what they forked. (7) ROS2 Jazzy + Ubuntu 22.04 compatibility 2026 - Humble vs Jazzy decision impact on FMI tooling. (8) Tool qualification for libcosim/farn/mlfmu - DNV-RP-0513 self-assurance evidence available? Recommendation: which subset is MUST-have for CCS AiP credibility vs nice-to-have, realistic incremental effort delta over schema-only Option B. Counter-evidence: any tool abandoned, showstopper bugs, or DNV/CCS rejected submissions?

entries.text: NTNU colav-simulator (github.com/ntnu-itk-autonomous-ship-lab/colav-simulator) AIS-driven scenario configuration and target ship instantiation: (1) How does colav-simulator load historical AIS data (NMEA / Kystverket / MarineTraffic / AISHub formats)? (2) How does it convert AIS tracks into scenario YAML / Cerberus-validated configs for target ships? (3) Region/time-window selection workflow — does the user pick a geographic bounding box + time slice, then the tool extracts encounter scenarios? (4) Target ship trajectory replay vs predicted/extrapolated continuation — how is target ship motion driven during simulation? (5) How does it integrate with seacharts / ENC chart display for visualization? Reference papers: Pedersen, Glomsrud et al. CCTA 2023; Hagen 2022 NTNU thesis. (6) What's the relationship between colav-simulator's AIS replay and DNV's ship-traffic-generator / maritime-schema TrafficSituation format — can AIS-derived scenarios be exported to maritime-schema YAML? (7) Concrete code-level patterns: scenario.yaml structure, AIS preprocessing pipeline, vessel ID/MMSI matching, gap interpolation, COG/SOG smoothing. (8) Comparable AIS-driven scenario tools: Norwegian Coastal Administration AIS dataset, Aeolus Ocean, MarineGym AIS replay capabilities. Goal: extract concrete engineering patterns to add 'AIS-driven scenario authoring' capability to a ROS2 Humble L3 TDL SIL framework, where users select AIS data slice → tool produces maritime-schema-compliant YAML scenario with own-ship + target-ship trajectories that can drive synthetic/ais_replay L1 mock modes.

entries.text: Web-based maritime SIL HMI with ENC chart integration and live simulation visualization for autonomous ship COLAV validation. Goal: extend an existing static HTML demo (radar-style mini display + CPA/TCPA panel + M1-M8 pipeline pulse + ASDR log) into a production-grade web HMI that (a) renders S-57 ENC chart background with land/depth/obstacles/buoys/TSS, (b) overlays own-ship + multiple AIS-driven target ships in real-time during SIL run, (c) replays recorded scenarios with timeline scrubber, (d) auto-generates GIF/PNG screenshots per scenario for evidence pack, (e) integrates with ROS2 Humble live data stream. Specific questions: (1) Open-source web ENC chart libraries: OpenSeaMap, NOAA ENC web viewer, Norwegian Kystdatahuset, OpenCPN web, MapLibre GL with maritime style — which is production-ready for S-57 raw data ingestion, what's the rendering performance for 1000+ vessel sprites? (2) S-57 to web tile pipeline: GDAL ogr2ogr + S-57 driver to GeoJSON / MVT / vector tiles — toolchain maturity? (3) Bridge HMI standards: IEC 62288 ECDIS display, S-mode (IMO MSC.1/Circ.1609), how much ECDIS visual grammar should an SIL HMI mimic to be CCS-credible — full ECDIS or just the situational awareness subset? (4) Foxglove Studio for ROS2 — does it support maritime ENC layers, is it suitable as the SIL operator console or just as a developer debug view? (5) NTNU seacharts library — its matplotlib-based architecture, can it be ported to web (likely no), what's the equivalent web-stack (OpenLayers + S-57 plugin)? (6) ROS2 → Web data bridge: rosbridge_server + roslibjs vs Foxglove WebSocket vs custom gRPC-Web — latency, scalability for 50Hz situational awareness updates, security model? (7) Replay + scrubber + GIF export workflow: how do projects like NTNU colav-simulator, PyGemini, Aeolus Ocean do recorded-scenario playback in web/desktop UIs, and how do they generate visual evidence artifacts? (8) Charting + decision rationale overlay: what UI patterns work for showing M6 COLREGs rule classification (R13/R14/R15 etc), L3 decision rationale (M5 BC-MPC right-turn 10 deg), Doer-Checker M7 verdict, ToR countdown — without overwhelming the operator? Reference: IEC 62288, IMO S-mode, NTNU colav-simulator visualization, DNV maritime-schema Apache Arrow output for replay. Recommendation: minimal-effort path to production-grade web HMI for L3 TDL SIL, what's MUST in Phase 1 D1.3b.3, what's Phase 2 D2.6 / Phase 3 D3.4 for full M8 HMI.
